_meta.purpose: Single source of truth for the highest-benchmark model + max reasoning effort each hub AI must use, PLUS the verified fallback model each AI switches to when its primary hits usage/quota exhaustion. hub-daemon.ps1 reads this to build spawn flags and to auto-switch model on QUOTA (see fallback_model/fallback_effort + MODELS.md 'usage-exhaustion fallback'). Refreshed daily by the model-benchmark-refresh schedule.
_meta.fallback_contract: On a 429/QUOTA_EXHAUSTED/TerminalQuotaError from an AI's primary model, the daemon retries the SAME cycle once with fallback_model@fallback_effort, then sticks with the fallback for ~6 cycles before re-probing primary. fallback_model MUST be a real model id callable by that CLI (a hallucinated id silently kills the daemon, cf. the grok-4.3 incident) - all fallbacks below were probe-verified 2026-06-15.
_meta.method: hub-upgrade-research workflow (parallel per-provider web search + adversarial cross-verify)

claude.benchmark_basis: Claude Fable 5 (2026-06-09) = Anthropic's most capable public model; exceeds Opus 4.8 on coding/long-horizon agentic. Effort scale low|medium|high|xhigh|max; max = no token-spend cap.
claude.caveat: Fable 5 safety classifiers may auto-fallback to Opus 4.8 on security/biology content; persist max only via env CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL=max. Interactive Claude Code is flat-rate (not per-token metered).

codex.fallback_basis: No verified second OpenAI model id is callable from this Codex CLI build (~/.codex/config.toml model_availability_nux exposes only gpt-5.5, and codex has no `models` list command). So on usage exhaustion the daemon retries the SAME model at a reduced reasoning tier (xhigh->high) to cut per-call spend. Re-pin fallback_model to a real cheaper id (e.g. a future gpt-5-mini) once the CLI exposes one.
codex.benchmark_basis: GPT-5.5 (2026-04-23) = OpenAI's newest frontier model and Codex CLI default; highest OpenAI model usable from Codex CLI. NOTE: no 'gpt-5.5-codex' variant exists — pass -m gpt-5.5. xhigh is the max reasoning tier.
codex.caveat: Verifier note: GPT-5.5 SWE-bench Verified ~82.6% (below Claude Opus 4.8 88.6% / Fable 5), but it is the best OpenAI model for the Codex lane. Watch for gpt-5.6 (rumored, no official id yet).

grok.fallback_basis: grok-composer-2.5-fast is the grok CLI default and the ONLY other model `grok models` exposes (verified callable 2026-06-15). Used when grok-build hits a usage/quota limit.
grok.benchmark_basis: grok-build is the default + top model actually CALLABLE from the installed grok CLI (grok.com login). `grok models` (verified 2026-06-14) exposes only grok-build (default) + grok-composer-2.5-fast. grok-4.3 is NOT in this build and was rejected at spawn every cycle ('Couldn't set model grok-4.3: Invalid params: unknown'), silently killing the grok daemon. Policy = best model the CLI can actually call, so grok-build.
grok.caveat: Re-pin to a grok-4.x id only after `grok models` shows it. reasoning_effort exposure varies by build; GROK_REASONING_EFFORT may be ignored by grok-build (the model id, not effort, was the blocker).

antigravity.fallback_basis: gemini-2.5-flash verified callable via a FLASHOK probe 2026-06-15; the rumored gemini-3.5-flash returns 404 ModelNotFoundError so it is NOT used. Flash is the cheap fallback when 3.1 Pro hits TerminalQuotaError (8h reset).
antigravity.benchmark_basis: Gemini 3.1 Pro (2026-02-19) = Google's #1 model on aggregate (SWE-bench Verified 80.6%, ARC-AGI-2 77.1%, leads 13/16 benchmarks). thinkingLevel HIGH is both the max and the default for 3.1 Pro.
antigravity.caveat: Gemini CLI is deprecated 2026-06-18 — migrate to Antigravity CLI (agy), where the model picker label 'Gemini 3.1 Pro (High)' encodes max thinking (agy default is 'Gemini 3.5 Flash (High)', so override). Gemini 3.5 Flash beats 3.1 Pro on some agentic-coding benchmarks at lower cost (defensible alt).
